title: WAGABOXÂ® Unit in Southwestern France Begins Injecting Biomethane
content: The WAGABOXÂ® unit at the  Monflanquin landfill in Southwestern France, has started operations, injecting biomethane into the local gas network. The project is a partnership between Waga Energy and Biogaz Monflanquin (a joint venture between Valorizon and AVERGIES), marking a major step in the regional energy transition. The Monflanquin WAGABOXÂ® unit will produce approximately 12 GWh per year, enough to supply 2,000 households. This biomethane will prevent roughly 3,200 tons of COâ‚‚ equivalent emissions annually. The locally produced biomethane can fuel Bio-CNG vehicles through the TÃ©mob station network, supporting decarbonization of heavy-duty transport.
